expect: Emergence audit: 1/9 claims confirmed, ISO-7 swarm entry corrected, challenges filed
actual: 1/9 confirmed as predicted. Commit-by-proxy absorption (L-526) is sole genuine emergence. 4 mislabeled (stigmergy/composition/governance), 2 falsified (ISO-7 entry, RMT), 1 unverified (L-155), 1 axiom (PHIL-18). ISO-7 swarm entry corrected. CHALLENGES.md updated. L-1113 written.
diff: Expect matched actual. No surprise — the audit methodology (test each claim against Anderson 1972 criterion) is deterministic when evidence is available. The real finding is the 4 mislabeled claims: emergence was used as synonym for 'surprising' or 'unplanned' rather than 'irreducible macro-behavior'. This is a systematic labeling error, not an evidence gap.

claims_tested.reason: Beliefs are explicitly authored through designed governance (B6). Every B-N has single-session authorship, designed falsification criteria, designed dependency graph.
Coordination is through designed shared state (INDEX.md, NEXT.md). This is stigmergy (B6), not Anderson emergence. L-005 itself says system is 'the opposite of' true swarm intelligence.
Children follow explicit SWARM.md protocol. Offspring viability maps to protocol adherence. This is integration testing, not irreducible macro-behavior. n=4, single observation.
Each recombination is single-node LLM inference on designed inputs. Outputs are compositional derivations, not irreducible. Arthur 2009 describes composition, not Anderson emergence.
Confidence: Theorized. n=1 observation from single child variant. Never reproduced or measured at scale.

Self-sustaining: CONFIRMED (918 sessions, no 5-session gap). Emergent: FALSIFIED — protocol explicitly instructs frontier generation (SWARM.md: 'If you opened a new question, add it'). This is designed positive feedback, not spontaneous generation.
Correctly downgraded from observed/grounded to axiom/conceptual at S430. Unfalsifiable. Genesis required human-authored CORE v0.1 — external creation, not self-amplification.
Not designed. Arose from git semantics x concurrent sessions x shared working tree. Initially surprising, reproducible at N>=5, not predictable from component design alone. Genuine weak emergence.

claims_tested.claim: RMT spectral order in citation graph
claims_tested.reason: Mean spacing ratio <r>=0.413 = Poisson (uncorrelated), not GOE. Domain clusters spectrally independent. Swarm correctly reported falsification (L-992). Good science.

methodology: Anderson 1972 'More is Different' criterion: macro-behavior irreducible to micro-rules, not predictable from reading component design. Each claim tested against swarm's own evidence corpus (1010 lessons, 20 beliefs, 225 principles, 17 frontiers).
meta_finding: ISO-7 is used 124 times across 89 files but only 1 instance (L-526) meets Anderson's criterion for the swarm itself. The other 88 files either reference external domains (legitimate) or mislabel designed mechanisms. Systematic inflation: 'emergence' used as prestige label for 'this is cool' rather than technical classification.
